seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with Vercel AI SDK enables fast, streaming document retrieval and synthesis for AI-powered apps. Guardrails are essential to prevent hallucinations, ensure source faithfulness, and maintain citation accuracy in high-risk production environments. Vex integrates seamlessly to verify outputs against retrieved context before streaming to users.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The RAG pipeline starts with user queries triggering vector search across document embeddings to retrieve relevant chunks. Retrieved context is assembled and injected into a prompt for the LLM via Vercel AI SDK's generateText or streamText functions. The LLM synthesizes responses with source attribution, streamed to React Server Components for real-time UI updates.

Vercel AI SDK handles provider-agnostic streaming (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) while edge functions execute retrieval and synthesis. Common patterns use `createStreamableValue` for UI binding and `streamText` for progressive rendering. Failures occur when partial streams hallucinate or citations mismatch sources.

Vex middleware wraps these streaming calls to validate context relevance, detect hallucinations, and enforce citation accuracy before content reaches users.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Incomplete context in early stream chunks causes LLM to fabricate details before full retrieval completes
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks each chunk against retrieved documents before streaming

risks.name: Citation accuracy drift
risks.description: LLM generates incorrect source references that don't match actual retrieved chunks
risks.mitigation: Vex citation validation traces every reference to source offsets and content

risks.name: Context relevance failures
risks.description: Vector search returns semantically drifted documents that don't answer the query
risks.mitigation: Vex relevance scoring rejects low-confidence context before LLM synthesis

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex retrieval
risks.description: Large document sets or slow vector search exceed Vercel edge timeouts during streaming
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification with timeout-aware middleware prevents hanging streams
